changes.explanation: Synchronizes the response-details toggle accessibility state with the panel visibility.

changes.modifiedContent:
import unittest

from tests.frontend_harness import run_dashboard


class ResponseDetailsAccessibilityTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_response_details_visibility_and_accessibility_state(self):
        opened = run_dashboard(["#detailsToggle"])
        self.assertFalse(opened["detailsHidden"])
        self.assertEqual(opened["detailsExpanded"], "true")

        toggle_closed = run_dashboard(["#detailsToggle", "#detailsToggle"])
        self.assertTrue(toggle_closed["detailsHidden"])
        self.assertEqual(toggle_closed["detailsExpanded"], "false")

        close_button_closed = run_dashboard(["#detailsToggle", "#detailsClose"])
        self.assertTrue(close_button_closed["detailsHidden"])
        self.assertEqual(close_button_closed["detailsExpanded"], "false")

        watching = run_dashboard(["#watchIncident"])
        self.assertEqual(watching["watching"], "true")
        self.assertEqual(watching["watchStatus"], "Watching incident")

        not_watching = run_dashboard(["#watchIncident", "#watchIncident"])
        self.assertEqual(not_watching["watching"], "false")
        self.assertEqual(not_watching["watchStatus"], "Not watching")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()
changes.explanation: Adds an independently runnable frontend-harness regression covering response-details state synchronization and watch behavior.
